storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Faint is a faded, hard-to-hatch seed.
People pity her for her flaws and love her for her weakness.
Knowing she chose to remain in the shell, now turned to dust, star dust.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In June of the 282nd year of the Alluvial Era, following an investigation application by the Mystia Treaty Committee, we dispatched a team of investigators to the New Leo area.
Since the meteor crash event a year ago, the abnormal mortality rate in the region has remained high.
Additionally, investigators discovered several mutated plants at the crash site. Instrumental analysis confirmed that these plants contained high levels of radiation, likely brought by the meteor.
However, no extraterrestrial minerals were found in the crater. Villagers testified that, on the third day after the meteor fell, a group of scientists in white coats cut and removed the meteor.
(Note: Suspected to belong to a civilian scientific organization; their identity remains unclear.)
After coordination between the Examination Committee and the local government, all residents were relocated, and the area was officially declared a no-living zone.
According to Mythag University's Physics Laboratory, the living ban must remain in effect for at least 80 years.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Faint saw the student secretly crying in the laboratory.
This wasn't her favorite student, but it was the one who liked her the most.
The female student never minded Faint's resistance and aloofness, nor was she afraid of Faint's uncontrollable abilities.
She would spend her living expenses on buying all the sweets, just because she was worried that Faint didn't have enough energy; she would wear the clunky protective suit just to comb Faint's tangled hair.
Faint thought... to repay her.
So, Faint did what she could.
—If she was just worried about failing exams and cried, then threatening others to give up their grades would be enough, wouldn't it?

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Among these sisters, one of them should never have been born.
On their home planet, a now desolate planet in what earthlings would call the Lyra constellation, Faint and Tinct spent half their lives exchanging bitter words.
But who is the unwanted child?
Faint, the older sister, is powerful yet uncontrollable; the life force she seizes seems to flow away like a river, leaving her only able to barely survive by licking the remaining dewdrops.
Tinct, the younger sister, is beautiful yet struggles to be independent; her life is as stubborn as a floating weed, yet rooted nowhere, causing her to revolve around her sister.
Perhaps they both believe they are the one who should not exist. It's just that Faint made her decision earlier—threatening self-termination, she forced her sister onto the journey of departure.
"Leave, Tinct. Don't let me hold you back."
"You shall sing among the stars, becoming the most eye-catching color."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Do I have the right to have wishes?
If so, can I become a part of my sister?
To make her whole, to make us whole.
That way, I can express "love" without reservation, and she can embrace "love" without distraction.
Dear Tinct... this is what I, as the firstborn child, should do for you, right?
